title: Observed modal clear behavior maps to desired active reset behavior

toRefs.title: Clear Filters resets active browse filters

summary: Observed runtime clears only modal-local filter controls, while desired UX resets active browse filter constraints.

preserved: The user has an explicit clear action.
changed: Clear action scope changes from modal-local controls to active browse constraints.
added: Reset result must be visible in browse state.
behaviorDelta.notesMd: This generalized mapping can be queried without reading prose in requirement rows.

recommendation.summary: Confirm desired active-browse reset behavior and align runtime.
recommendation.rationaleMd: The visible label implies active filter reset from the user perspective.

alternatives.summary: Keep modal-local clear behavior and rename the action.
alternatives.tradeoffsMd: Preserves runtime behavior but requires UX copy and probably another reset action.

recommendation.consequencesMd: Implementation should not silently preserve modal-local behavior if desired UX is confirmed.

confirmation.notesMd: User confirmation is needed before treating the behavior change as governing.

implementationGate.reason: Desired behavior change is pending confirmation.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.
